task.title: Worker prompts
task.description: Write worker prompt sub-files embedded by commands/orchestrate.md into agent spawn prompts. system.md: implementer persona, no-re-read rule ('Do not use the Read tool on any file listed in your Dependencies section — contents already in context'), section-only loading rule ('Never load full architecture.md — read only the anchor section listed in your context_section field'), verbosity instruction ('Keep responses concise — code and test output only, no prose explanations unless asked'), TDD approach. context_bundle.md: bundle format spec and interpretation guide. testing_guide.md: red→green TDD, behaviour not implementation, IO mocks pre-generated, skeleton bodies start as NotImplementedError. Each file ≤ 150 lines.

Orchestrator prompts: planning.md (milestone decomposition format from architecture.md). task.title: Handoff skill — Claude provider
task.description: Update commands/handoff.md (the existing working handoff skill). Must flush current session state to the living document (architecture.md for oracle sessions, execution_plan.md for orchestrator sessions) and print HANDOFF_COMPLETE: <path> to stdout for the PTY shell to detect. Skills also emit HANDOFF RECOMMENDED: <reason> proactively at natural stopping points. Compact format rule: when writing architecture.md or execution_plan.md, use tables and bullet points only — no prose paragraphs. Uses .agentflow/ paths. Gemini provider deferred.

task.title: Inline .idx generation in orchestrate skill
task.description: Update commands/orchestrate.md pre-spawn section to generate .idx files for every file in each task's reads list: Python files → ast-based symbol extraction (function names, class names, class methods in ClassName.method:start-end format); Markdown files → grep-based H2/H3 header extraction (format: ## Header:start-end). Write .idx files to ~/.agentflow/cache/<sha256-of-project-root>/index/<mirrored-path>.idx. Update commands/worker/system.md to add targeted reads instruction: before reading any file in the reads list, grep the .idx for the needed symbol, then use Read(offset, limit) instead of reading the full file. Fallback: if .idx absent or symbol not found, read full file. Both changes are prompt/instruction additions only — no Python code.

unit: commands/orchestrate.md pre-spawn section includes .idx generation step for Python files (ast)
commands/orchestrate.md pre-spawn section includes .idx generation step for Markdown files (grep H2/H3)
commands/orchestrate.md specifies cache path ~/.agentflow/cache/<hash>/index/
commands/worker/system.md includes targeted reads instruction (grep .idx then Read(offset, limit))
commands/worker/system.md includes fallback to full file read when .idx absent or symbol not found

task.acceptance_criteria: orchestrate.md generates .idx files in pre-spawn for py/md reads; worker/system.md instructs targeted reads via .idx with fallback; no Python code added

task.title: Symbol index — Python parser
task.description: AST-based parser for Python files. Define IndexEntry dataclass in agentflow/indexer/__init__.py: fields name (str), kind (str), start_line (int), end_line (int), signature (str | None). python_parser.py exposes parse(path: Path) → list[IndexEntry]. Extracts: top-level functions (kind='function'), top-level classes (kind='class'), and class methods in ClassName.method format (kind='method'). Include signature string for functions and methods. Skip files with fewer than 50 lines (return []). No external deps beyond stdlib + ast. Unreadable or unparseable files return [] without raising.
task.acceptance_criteria: parse returns correct IndexEntry list for a fixture Python file with functions, classes, and methods; files < 50 lines skipped; no external deps

task.title: Symbol index — Markdown parser
task.description: Regex-based parser for Markdown files. markdown_parser.py exposes parse(path: Path) → list[IndexEntry]. Extracts H2 (##) and H3 (###) section headers with line ranges. name includes ## prefix. kind='section'. end_line = line before next header at same/higher level or EOF. Skip files < 50 lines. No external deps beyond stdlib. Unreadable files return [] without raising.
task.acceptance_criteria: parse returns correct IndexEntry list; H2/H3 extracted; end_line correct; files < 50 lines skipped; no external deps

task.title: Symbol index — index manager and brownfield scanner
task.description: index_manager.py: manages the cache at ~/.agentflow/cache/<sha256-of-project-root>/index/<mirrored-path>.idx. Exposes update(path, contents) → None (write index entry), lookup(path, symbol) → IndexEntry|None (targeted read), get_index(path) → list[IndexEntry] (all entries for a file). Cache miss regenerates from file contents deterministically. brownfield_scanner.py: walk project tree on first load, index all files meeting type+size thresholds, populate cache. Exposes scan(project_root) → int (files indexed). Both modules are idempotent — safe to run twice.

unit: update writes .idx file at correct cache path
lookup returns correct IndexEntry for known symbol
lookup returns None for unknown symbol
cache miss triggers regeneration from file contents
update is idempotent — running twice produces same result
brownfield scan returns count of files indexed
brownfield scan skips files below size threshold
brownfield scan is idempotent
integration: scan project fixture tree, then lookup symbol — returns correct entry

security_constraints: cache path must be outside project tree — validate it resolves under ~/.agentflow/
task.acceptance_criteria: lookup returns correct entry after update; brownfield scan indexes all eligible files; cache path never inside project tree
task.result: All unit and integration tests passing successfully (58 tests passed in total, with 96% coverage on the indexer package)

task.title: Oracle skill — remove CV notification (IP protection)

title: CLAUDE.md — universal .idx reading protocol
description: Add a 'Reading protocol' section to CLAUDE.md as a project-wide standing instruction: before reading any file, check for ~/.agentflow/cache/<sha256(cwd)>/index/<path>.idx; if found, use offset=start limit=end-start+1 for the target symbol/section; if absent, file is < 50 lines or not yet indexed — read the full file with no offset/limit. This rule applies to all Claude sessions in this project regardless of which skill is active (oracle, orchestrate, handoff, worker, or none). No per-skill repetition needed — one rule, one place.

unit: CLAUDE.md contains a Reading protocol section
CLAUDE.md specifies .idx lookup path using sha256(cwd)
CLAUDE.md specifies offset=start limit=end-start+1 when .idx found
CLAUDE.md specifies full read fallback when .idx absent
CLAUDE.md explains absence means file < 50 lines or not yet indexed

acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md Reading protocol section present; rule applies universally across all skills; consistent with worker/system.md convention
